task_completion.description: Plot the three specified profile points for the cooling tower.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for points (0, 46), (85, 26), and (114, 27.5) defined in the GeoGebra Algebra view or visible in the Graphics view.

task_completion.description: Create a polynomial function passing through the three profile points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a polynomial function (e.g., created using FitPoly) in the Algebra view that passes through the three defined points.

task_completion.description: Generate a 3D surface of revolution by rotating the function around the x-axis for 0 ≤ x ≤ 114.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 3D Graphics view showing a cooling tower shape (hyperboloid-like) and a Surface command in the Algebra view rotating the function around the x-axis (or y=0) restricted to the domain [0, 114].

task_completion.description: Calculate the internal volume of the cooling tower.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an integral calculation in the Algebra view representing the volume of revolution (e.g., Integral(π * f², 0, 114)), which should evaluate to approximately 363,137.

task_completion.description: Include a text annotation showing the capacity in Olympic swimming pools.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text object in the Graphics view formatted exactly as 'Capacity: [number] pools'. The number should be approximately 145 (calculated as volume / 2500).
Look for the GeoGebra window title showing 'cooling_tower.ggb' or a terminal/file explorer window confirming the file exists in the ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra GUI to build the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GeoGebra application open with the model being constructed in the Algebra and Graphics/3D Graphics views, not just terminal commands creating a file.

integrity.description: The volume and pool capacity are derived from actual mathematical calculations in GeoGebra.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Algebra view should show the mathematical steps (e.g., Integral command, division by 2500) rather than just a static text box with a hardcoded number.

integrity.description: The agent did not blindly copy incorrect metadata values for the pool capacity.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text annotation should show the mathematically correct value of approximately 145 pools, rather than a hallucinated value between 150-170 pools that might have been leaked from faulty metadata.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task models the Drax Power Station cooling tower. Verified points: Base (0, 46), Top (114, 27.5). 1 Olympic swimming pool = 2500 m³. IMPORTANT: A quadratic polynomial fitted to the three given points yields an actual volume of approximately 363,137 m³, which equals approximately 145.3 Olympic pools. Accept values around 145 pools for the text annotation. Do not penalize the agent if the value is ~145 instead of the 150-170 range, as the mathematical calculation supersedes any conflicting metadata.
